These three addresses make that very easy to do.

No. 01
Breakfast & Healthy Lunch · All-Day Café · Design Store

Casa & Bottega

The most beautiful morning in Positano starts here
About the Venue
✦ All Pastries Baked Fresh Daily · Pottery by the Nonnas

Casa & Bottega is Positano's most charming all-day café — part design store, part wellness kitchen, all atmosphere. Family-owned since 2012 and run with deep Italian pride, every pastry on the counter is made from scratch that morning, and the hand-decorated ceramics and pottery you'll admire on the shelves and tables are handmade by local Nonnas. The menu leans clean and intentional: superfood bowls, house-made granola, fresh-baked sweets, pressed juices, and seriously good matcha drinks served in their signature ribbed sea-glass green glassware. The interior — warm wood, trailing greenery, artisanal ceramics in the same palette as the glassware — is the most aesthetically considered room in Positano. Everything here is made with the kind of care you'd expect from a place that looks this good.

What to Order
  • Iced Matcha Latte
    Ceremonial-grade matcha layered over cold oat milk in their beautiful ridged glassware. Exceptional quality — the standard against which all Amalfi Coast café drinks should be measured.
  • Freshly Baked Pastries of the Day
    Made from scratch every single morning by the kitchen team. The banana chocolate bread with caramelized bananas is legendary. Ask what came out of the oven that day — it changes with the season and the whim of whoever started baking first.
  • Tortellini al Pesto di Zucchine
    House-made tortellini bathed in a silky zucchini pesto, topped with crispy fried zucchini coins and aged Parmigiano. Light, verdant, and genuinely remarkable for a lunch that doesn't weigh you down.
  • Green Mojito (Non-Alcoholic)
    Cucumber, lime, fresh mint, and pressed apple juice over ice. A deeply refreshing midday drink for Positano's summer heat.
Practical Information
◷
Hours
9:00am – 3:00pm · Breakfast & lunch only · No reservations, walk-in
✕
Closed
Closed Mondays & Tuesdays — plan accordingly
✦
Address
Viale Pasitea 100, Positano · +39 089 875225
🏺
The Shop
Hand-decorated ceramics, cups, artisanal fabrics — all handmade by local Nonnas and strictly Made in Italy. A perfect piece of Positano to bring home.
€
Price Range
€€ · Moderate — exceptional value for Positano
Insider Note
Arrive early for the best pastry selection — they sell out. The ceramics on your table? You can buy them. The Nonnas' pottery makes a far better souvenir than anything from the tourist shops on the main strip. Don't leave without looking at the shop.
No. 02
Dinner · Family-Owned Since 1958 · Michelin Selected

Da Vincenzo

Positano's most beloved dinner table, unchanged since 1958
About the Venue

If Positano has a soul, it eats at Da Vincenzo. Family-owned since the grandfather opened the doors in 1958, this Michelin-selected trattoria on Viale Pasitea has outlasted trends, tourist waves, and every attempt at being replaced by somewhere shinier. The menu centers on the freshest fish of the day, handmade pasta, and a wine list that suggests someone genuinely cares which bottle you open. The few outdoor tables on the pavement are the most coveted seats in Positano on any given evening.

Do Not Skip · The First Thing to Order
The Olive Oil — Quite Possibly the Best in Italy
Before the pasta, before the wine, before anything else — ask for the bread and olive oil. Da Vincenzo's house olive oil is extraordinary: grassy, peppery, deeply complex, the kind that makes you question every other olive oil you've ever tasted. It will genuinely stop you mid-conversation.
↳
They know exactly how good it is — and they'll only bring a small pour unless you ask for more. Ask for more. Then ask again. It's so good one guest went back the next day specifically to purchase a bottle to bring home. The shop at the front sells it.
Must Order
  • Tortellini al Pesto ★ House Favourite
    The standout dish of the menu. Silky house-made tortellini in a bright, herbaceous pesto that somehow manages to feel both light and deeply satisfying. The dish that earns a second visit. Do not hesitate.
  • Linguine alle Vongole
    Fresh clams, garlic, white wine, olive oil, parsley. No cream, no shortcuts. The standard by which you judge every other vongole on the Amalfi Coast.
  • Ravioli al Pomodoro & Basilico
    House-made egg pasta in a concentrated San Marzano tomato sauce, finished with a vivid drizzle of basil oil. Simple, brilliant, the kind of dish that ruins supermarket pasta forever.
  • Alici 'Ndorate e Fritte
    Fried anchovies in a delicate flour batter — the Campanian coastal starter at its very best. Order immediately upon sitting down.
  • Buffalo Cheesecake or Tiramisù
    Both made in-house. The tiramisù reportedly converted at least one non-believer. The cheesecake, made with local buffalo milk, is something else entirely — creamy, tangy, unforgettable.
Practical Information
◷
Hours
Lunch 12:15–2:30pm (except Tuesdays) · Dinner 6:15–10:30pm daily
✕
Closed
Closed Tuesday lunches · Open for dinner on Tuesdays
✦
Address
Viale Pasitea 172, Positano · +39 089 875128 · davincenzo.it
€
Price Range
€€–€€€ · ~€40–70pp with wine · Excellent value for the quality
↳
Reservations
Essential — book up to 60 days in advance in summer. Request an outdoor table. Guests begin queuing by 6:00pm.
🛒
To Take Home
Their olive oil is available to purchase. After tasting it with the bread, you will want to. Buy two bottles.
★
Recognition
Michelin Guide Selected · Family-run since 1958 · Perennial favourite
Insider Note
Order the bread and olive oil the moment you sit — do not wait for someone to offer it. Ask for more oil than they bring. Then ask again. Ask for Raphael, who has served tables here for years and knows the wine list better than anyone. A glass of Falanghina with the tortellini. You will want to come back the next night.
No. 03
Cocktails · Sunset · Panoramic Rooftop · Hotel Villa Franca

GOLD Sky Bar

360° views over the entire bay — Positano's best-kept rooftop
About the Venue

Perched at the highest point in Positano on the rooftop of the boutique Hotel Villa Franca, GOLD Sky Bar is the town's finest panoramic cocktail experience — and one of its best-kept secrets. While Franco's Bar at Le Sirenuse draws the loudest crowd (and rightly so), GOLD offers something rarer: the same jaw-dropping views of the bay, the Lattari Mountains, and the pastel cascade of Positano houses, with significantly more breathing room and a far more intimate atmosphere. On a clear evening, the panorama stretches from Amalfi village to the mythological Li Galli islands and across the cobalt Gulf of Salerno. It is, simply, the most beautiful view in Positano.

What to Drink
  • The Golden Rose
    GOLD's signature — gin, fresh grapefruit juice, rose cordial, tonic water. Light, floral, perfectly tuned to a Positano sunset. Order this first.
  • Champagne by the Glass
    The rooftop doubles as a champagne bar in high season. A glass of millésimé at golden hour, with the sea going amber in front of you, is the Amalfi Coast experience at its most distilled.
  • Spritz & Aperitivo Selection
    Expert mixologists craft Campari, Aperol, and signature spritzes with local citrus and Amalfi lemon liqueur. Order at the precise moment the sun touches the horizon.
Practical Information
◷
Best Time
Golden hour — arrive 45–60 min before sunset for the best terrace position
✕
Season
Open seasonally · Confirm dates with the hotel directly in shoulder season
✦
Location
HVF Villa Franca · Viale Pasitea 318, Positano · Open to non-hotel guests
👁
The View
True 360° — Bay of Salerno, Li Galli islands, Amalfi coastline, Positano rooftops & the Lattari Mountains
€
Price Range
€€€ · Cocktails €18–25 · Worth every euro for the setting
GOLD vs. Franco's Bar
Franco's Bar at Le Sirenuse is the legendary Positano aperitivo — absolutely worth visiting, a genuine icon. But GOLD offers a calmer, less-crowded alternative with an arguably more complete panorama. Do both if you can. Franco's for the scene; GOLD for the silence and the view.
Also Highly Recommended

Franco's Bar
at Le Sirenuse

Franco's Bar is the iconic Positano aperitivo experience — the legendary terrace bar of Le Sirenuse hotel, beloved since the 1940s and still one of the most beautifully positioned bars on the Amalfi Coast. Expect impeccably made Negronis, Champagne, and a stylish crowd watching the bay turn amber at dusk. The vibe is glamorous, vibrant, and quintessentially Italian Riviera. It's busier and more social than GOLD — which is precisely its charm. Order the Negroni, take the terrace table, and feel the Mediterranean sink into you.

Tip: Arrive at aperitivo hour (6–7pm) and book the terrace in advance during July and August. The cocktail list is shorter than GOLD's but the classics are flawless.

"Two of the most beautiful cocktail hours on the Amalfi Coast — and they are ten minutes' walk from each other. There is no reason not to do both."
Franco's Bar · Le Sirenuse Hotel · Via Cristoforo Colombo 30
